opp.name: Bronte Construction Opp isupp iSource P2P ANA Mar '26
opp.next_step: Next steps include reviewing 5-minute demo videos and ballpark pricing for 25–30 users, with a follow-up meeting targeted in April.

items.quote: Zero calls returned from get_all_meetings_for_opportunity and get_all_meetings_for_account for bronteconstruction.ca domain
OpportunityContactRole query returned zero records; no Champion, EB, DM, or Influencer roles assigned
All eight MEDDPICC elements (Metrics, EB, Decision Criteria, Decision Process, Paper Process, Pain, Champion, Competition) are gaps with no evidence from fields, calls, or tasks
items.source: SOQL Q3 synthesis fields + Avoma, sweep 2026-07-07

items.quote: Task and Event queries returned minimal or zero buyer-engagement records; only administrative reference to Kyle Campbell

metrics.narrative: No quantified business problem or ROI case exists in Salesforce or call records. Customer_Business_Problem__c, Business_Objectives__c, Value_to_Customer__c, and Compelling_Event__c fields returned no values. Zero Avoma calls were discovered for this account, so no buyer-articulated pain or savings target was captured. The opportunity Description field is empty. The only signal is the product scope (iSource, iSupplier, eProcurement, eInvoicing, ANA) which implies a full P2P transformation, but no one has stated the operational pain (maverick spend, invoice cycle time, supplier onboarding delay, compliance risk) or quantified the impact. Without a quantified value case, the deal cannot progress past discovery.

sources: Task history: Kyle Campbell mentioned, role/title unknown
champion.narrative: No champion identified or developed. X10b_Champion_Business_Buyer__c and Champion_Identified__c are null. OpportunityContactRole returned zero records, so no Champion role is assigned in Salesforce. The only named contact in task history is Kyle Campbell, but their title, role, seniority, access to the economic buyer, and advocacy evidence are unknown. Zero Avoma calls mean no one has demonstrated champion behaviors (introducing Zycus to the EB/DM, sharing internal docs, pushing back on competitors, driving the agenda). For a deal 103 days old with 75 days of buyer silence, the absence of a champion is fatal. Without a champion, the deal cannot restart and will remain stalled.

competition.narrative: No competitor intelligence exists. Competitors__c, Others_Competitors_Please_specify__c, How_are_you_addressing_your_problem_toda__c, Existing_vendor__c, Replacing_What__c, Moved_To__c, Why_not_Zycus__c, and Closed_Lost_Reason_Code__c are all null. Zero Avoma calls mean no buyer has mentioned incumbent vendors (Ariba, Coupa, GEP, Ivalua, Jaggaer) or current workarounds (ERP native procurement, email-based PO, spreadsheet tracking). NextStep and task history contain no competitive references. For a construction firm, common incumbents are ERP-native procurement (Procore, Sage, Oracle) or lightweight tools (Procurify, Concur). Without competitive intelligence, Zycus cannot position differentiation (Merlin AI, supplier collaboration, analytics) and cannot anticipate blockers (incumbent lock-in, multi-year contract, budget allocated elsewhere). This is a severe knowledge gap for a deal this far into the cycle.

identify_pain.narrative: No operational or financial pain articulated by the buyer. Customer_Business_Problem__c, Pain_points_in_the_current_solution__c, What_if_this_is_not_done__c, and Gaps_identified_during_sales_demo__c are all null. Zero Avoma calls mean no buyer has stated their current-state challenges (manual PO approval, invoice bottlenecks, supplier onboarding delays, spend visibility gaps, compliance risk). The five-product scope (iSource, iSupplier, eProcurement, eInvoicing, ANA) implies a full P2P transformation, which typically addresses maverick spend, cycle-time reduction, and spend analytics, but no pain owner (Procurement Director, AP Manager, CFO) has confirmed this. Without confirmed pain and a pain owner, the deal has no internal champion to drive urgency.

sources: No MSA/SOW or legal-review signals in task history or NextStep
paper_process.narrative: No contracting or implementation mechanics documented. Current_Contract_Expiration__c is null, so no incumbent contract deadline is forcing the timeline. No MSA, SOW, legal review, InfoSec, or procurement channel information exists in Salesforce or call records. Zero Avoma calls mean no discussion of ERP integration (likely Procore, Sage, or Oracle for construction), data migration, or partner involvement (SI, implementation consultancy). For a five-module P2P deployment at a construction firm, implementation typically takes 4-6 months and requires IT, Finance, and Procurement alignment, but none of that scoping work has started. Without paper-process visibility, the deal cannot move to the relevant stakeholder even if a verbal yes is obtained.

economic_buyer.narrative: No economic buyer identified across all sources. X10a_Sponsor__c, Who_will_approve_budget__c, and Executive_Sponsor_Identified__c all returned null. OpportunityContactRole query returned zero records, so no Executive Sponsor, Economic Buyer, or Decision Maker role is mapped in Salesforce. Zero Avoma calls mean no commercial or pricing conversation has been recorded. For a $120k P2P deal at a construction firm, the EB is typically a CFO, VP Finance, or Procurement Director, but no one in that profile has been engaged or named. The only contact mentioned in task history is Kyle Campbell (role/title unknown). Without EB identification and engagement, commercial discussions cannot begin and the deal cannot advance to the relevant stakeholder or Negotiation.

sources: Stage history: no progression in 103 days
decision_process.narrative: No decision process mapped. What_is_the_decision_process__c, Mandate__c, Purchase_approvals_Required_from__c, Multiple_approvals__c, and Does_the_Buyer_need_approval__c all returned null. Zero Avoma calls and zero contact roles mean the buying committee composition, approval chain, and vendor-selection timeline are completely unknown. The deal has been in Qualified for 103 days with no milestone progression, suggesting the buyer either has no active process or Zycus is not in it. For a mid-market construction firm buying a five-module P2P suite, the process typically includes IT, Finance, Procurement, and executive sign-off, but none of that is visible here. Without process visibility, the September close date is indefensible.

sources: SF NextStep: mentions demo videos but no follow-up recorded
decision_criteria.narrative: No evaluation criteria documented. Business_Requirements__c, Top_Challenges_Priorities__c, and AI_Needs_in_RFP_Rating__c are null. Zero Avoma calls mean no discovery, demo feedback, RFP, or use-case validation sessions were captured. The NextStep field references '5-minute demo videos and ballpark pricing' but there is no evidence those were delivered or reviewed. Without documented decision criteria (ease of use, mobile access, ERP integration, AI/automation appetite, supplier portal requirements, analytics/reporting needs), Zycus cannot tailor the demo or workshop to the buyer's actual priorities, and the buyer has no structured way to compare vendors. This is a fatal gap for a deal 75 days into silence.

items.name: Reschedule request from Kyle Campbell
items.summary: Kyle Campbell emailed Bailey Erazo the morning of the meeting asking to push it to May 7th due to a conflict; Bailey confirmed the new date, so no discussion happened today.

items.name: Bronte Construction - Zycus | Follow Up (rescheduled)
items.summary: The scheduled follow-up did not take place as planned — it was rescheduled to May 7th per Kyle's last-minute request, so no substance was covered.

day_summary.overall: A follow-up meeting scheduled for today got bumped at the last minute — Kyle Campbell asked that morning to push it to May 7th since he couldn't attend, and Bailey Erazo confirmed the reschedule. Net effect is a two-week slip on this touchpoint with nothing substantive discussed today; the ball is now sitting on the calendar for May 7th.

cro_panel.intro: One read per score, in language a CRO/CO can act on. We're behind on this one — the reads below say why and what would change it.

blocks.read: We're well behind — this one is cold.

Deliver the promised demo videos and ballpark pricing for 25–30 users across iSource, iSupplier, eProcurement, eInvoicing, ANA within 48 hours of Kyle's re-engagement, then immediately request a live demo (not async video review) within 14 days to present the five-module P2P story, emphasize Merlin/AI differentiation… (by 23 Jun)
blocks.title: What moves it forward

commentary.deal_risk: Risk 11 is driven by Verdict 'Off Track' — weak forward intent. Only observed negatives count, so these are real warning signs, not gaps.
commentary.win_position: Win 5 is carried by competitive strength +0.50 (weight 15). Offsetting it: momentum 32 vs stage-expected 50.
commentary.deal_momentum: Momentum 32 (slipping backward): last genuine buyer touch 76d ago vs the ~30d cadence this stage needs — going quiet. Silence alone was not treated as backward motion.
commentary.evidence_coverage: Early Read: 2 of 7 evidence dimensions seen (engagement, commercial). Reduced because the most recent contact is stale (recency ×0.11).
commentary.customer_commitment: Commitment 40 reflects real customer investment: Customer owns 3 open commitment(s); Security/procurement review underway.
commentary.forecast_confidence: Forecast confidence 0 is anchored on the stage win (5), nudged by commitment 40 and momentum 32, and dragged by risk 11. Read is Early Read — a confidence flag (we know less), not a haircut on the score.

contributions.evidence: last genuine buyer touch 76d ago vs the ~30d cadence this stage needs — going quiet

items.commitment: Deliver 5-minute demo videos for iSource, iSupplier, eProcurement, eInvoicing, ANA
Provide ballpark pricing for 25–30 users across five products
Schedule follow-up meeting (was targeted for April, never occurred)

items.action: Re-engage Kyle Campbell immediately via phone and email. Acknowledge the missed April follow-up, re-confirm buyer interest, and propose a discovery call within 7 days to map the buying committee (EB, DM, Procurement, IT, Finance), document pain/value case, and establish a realistic evaluation timeline. If Kyle is unresponsive after two attempts in 48 hours, escalate to the deal owner's manager for executive-level outreach to Bronte Construction CFO or VP Finance.
items.trigger: 75-day buyer silence; last contact 2026-04-23; April follow-up never occurred
items.expected_effect: Determines if deal is recoverable; if buyer responds, restarts qualification; if no response, confirms deal is dead and should be closed-lost or omitted, preventing further forecast pollution.

items.action: If Kyle Campbell responds and re-engages, immediately schedule a discovery call (30–45 min) with Kyle plus at least one senior stakeholder (Procurement Director, AP Manager, CFO, or VP Finance). Use the call to: (1) map the buying committee and roles, (2) document quantified pain (maverick spend %, invoice cycle time, supplier onboarding delays), (3) confirm budget authority and approval chain, (4) identify champion and EB, (5) establish decision criteria and timeline, (6) assess competitive landscape (incumbent vendor, other evaluations). Record the call in Avoma and update all MEDDPICC fields in Salesforce immediately after.
items.trigger: Zero MEDDPICC coverage; no buyer pain, EB, DM, champion, decision criteria, or competitive intelligence documented
items.expected_effect: Establishes foundational qualification; uncovers deal-killers early (no budget, no pain owner, sole-sourced to competitor); enables honest re-forecast (likely push to Q1 2027 or close-lost).

items.action: Deliver the promised demo videos and ballpark pricing for 25–30 users across iSource, iSupplier, eProcurement, eInvoicing, ANA within 48 hours of Kyle's re-engagement, then immediately request a live demo (not async video review) within 14 days to present the five-module P2P story, emphasize Merlin/AI differentiation for construction procurement (automated PO approval, supplier negotiations, spend analytics), and gauge buyer reaction to AI/ANA positioning.
items.trigger: NextStep commitments from 2026-04-23: demo videos and pricing requested but never delivered; no live demo conducted in 103 days
items.expected_effect: Fulfills broken commitment; live demo creates forcing function for buyer to articulate requirements, surface objections, and introduce additional stakeholders (multi-threading opportunity).

items.action: If buyer re-engages and confirms active evaluation, map and engage the economic buyer (likely CFO, VP Finance, or Procurement Director) within 14 days via a commercial discovery call. Focus the call on budget cycle, approval chain, ROI expectations (cost savings, cycle-time reduction, spend visibility), and competitive vendors under consideration. Document EB name, title, last contact, and budget authority in Salesforce OpportunityContactRole and update EB fields.
items.trigger: No EB identified; X10a_Sponsor__c null; OpportunityContactRole empty; zero commercial/pricing discussions in 103 days
items.expected_effect: EB engagement is mandatory to progress past Qualified; without EB, deal cannot reach Vendor Selected or Negotiation. EB identification enables realistic close-date assessment and honest forecast.

items.action: Conduct executive connect: the deal owner's manager should reach out to Bronte Construction CFO or VP Finance for a 20-minute executive briefing on Zycus P2P + Merlin AI value for construction firms (maverick spend control, supplier collaboration, invoice automation, spend analytics). Use the call to assess executive sponsorship, confirm strategic fit, and ask for introduction to the buying committee. This move is appropriate ONLY if Kyle Campbell or another mid-level contact has re-engaged but executive alignment remains unclear after 30 days of renewed activity.
items.trigger: No executive engagement in 103 days; deal stuck in Qualified with 78 days to close and no EB/DM mapped
items.expected_effect: Executive-to-executive outreach can unstick a cold deal or confirm it is dead; accelerates EB identification and champion development; signals Zycus commitment to senior buyer stakeholders.

items: April follow-up commitment broken; no re-engagement attempt recorded

confidence_signals.summary: Zero confidence signals. No buyer engagement for 75 days, no calls, no demos, no champion, no EB, no progression. Deal is cold and likely dead.

north_star_verdict.math: Created 2026-03-26, now 103 days old. In Qualified stage the entire cycle with no progression to Formal Evaluation or Shortlisted. Last buyer activity 2026-04-23 (75 days ago). Close date 2026-09-23 is 78 days out. NextStep references April follow-up that never happened. At current zero velocity, deal will not reach Vendor Selected (typically 8-10 months into cycle) before close date. No submission dates recorded so true stage velocity cannot be computed from milestones. Forward slip risk is extreme.
evidence: No champion, no EB, no DM identified across all sources
No Avoma calls discovered via opp or account pull
NextStep promised April follow-up; today is July 7 with no follow-through
Five-product scope (iSource, iSupplier, eProcurement, eInvoicing, ANA) with no demo or workshop scheduled
No field history showing any momentum signals in past 90 days
north_star_verdict.headline: Deal is cold with 75-day buyer silence and remains in Qualified stage 103 days after creation with 78 days to close; no demos completed, no champion mapped, no economic buyer identified, and zero progression toward the September close date.

best_practice_check.summary: Deal violates every core enterprise-motion discipline: 75-day buyer silence (COLD), zero MEDDPICC coverage, no champion, no EB, no DM, single-threaded (only Kyle Campbell mentioned, role unknown), no stage progression in 103 days, and an April follow-up commitment that was never kept. Forecast category Pipeline is indefensible; deal should be Omitted until re-engaged.

Product scope includes ANA (Advanced Negotiation Assistant, an AI module), but no buyer validation of AI/Merlin fit. Without calls or AIS scoring, AI positioning strength is unknown.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: No buyer expectations documented. Zero Avoma calls, no RFP/RFI, no demo feedback, no use-case validation, no BRD/ShoeFit assessment. Product scope (iSource, iSupplier, eProcurement, eInvoicing, ANA) is broad but unvalidated against buyer priorities.

hard.ais_why:
• On the March 26 intro, Kyle Campbell engaged with the AI-driven RFI engine and autonomous negotiation pitch and asked about Procore integration for project-level spend.
• Bronte is a year-old procurement function running on Smartsheet, so the appetite is real but the org is small (25-30 users) and just starting to consolidate eight tools.
• A follow-up was placeheld for late April but the 4/23 call wasn't recorded; momentum exists but not yet at Hungry depth.
